summary: Phase 3-2: activeSubscriptions tracking bug in ElectronPluginTransport — cleanup closure mismatched from stored reference
details: plugin-transport.ts on() stores `unsub` from api.pluginOn() in activeSubscriptions, but the cleanup closure calls `activeSubscriptions.delete(cleanup)` — cleanup is not what was stored. Manual unsub followed by destroy() calls unsub() twice. ipcRenderer.removeListener is idempotent, so no crash, but tracking is inconsistent.

summary: Phase 3-2: main-process handler registry has no lifecycle cleanup — dead plugin handlers persist
details: plugin-transport-ipc.ts module-level Maps (messageHandlers, invokeHandlers) have no lifecycle hook. If a plugin's lifecycle ends without calling unsub, handlers remain registered and fire on channel reuse. Unlike renderer-side transport (has destroy()), main-process side has no cleanup mechanism.

summary: Phase 3-2: pluginOff is no-op violating PluginTransport interface contract
details: preload/index.ts pluginOff is explicitly no-op. PluginTransport interface declares off() — a caller who doesn't hold the unsub reference from on() cannot unsubscribe, leaking the handler forever.
